description_long: This object is a pair of needle-nose pliers featuring elongated, tapered jaws ideal for precision gripping and manipulation in tight spaces. The handles are ergonomically curved and coated in a dark, rubberized material with yellow accent rings near the pivot, providing both comfort and slip resistance. The jaws are constructed from a metallic material, likely steel, and are designed to meet at a fine point for detailed work such as bending wire or holding small components. The pivot joint is a standard fixed type, allowing the jaws to open and close smoothly without adjustable width. The overall construction suggests durability and suitability for tasks in urban environments such as electrical work, repairs, or crafting. No visible branding or logos are present on the exterior surfaces.
description: A pair of needle-nose pliers with long, pointed steel jaws and curved, rubber-coated handles with yellow accents. Designed for precision gripping and manipulation in tight spaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the curved, rubberized handles and the narrow, pointed jaws meeting at the tip.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the slim profile of the jaws and the ergonomic curvature of the handles.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the open handles and the alignment of the jaws from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the length of the handles and the fine taper of the jaws.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, ergonomic handles with black rubberized coating and yellow accent rings near the pivot.
attribute_jaw_design: Long, needle-nose jaws with a fine tapered point, suitable for precision work; likely feature a lightly serrated gripping surface.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed pivot joint, standard single-axis mechanism without adjustable jaw width.
attribute_grip_pattern: Rubberized handles with a smooth, matte finish for enhanced grip.
